frame: Labor-market wage paths feed lifetime NPV only through earnings/tax proxies — not 1:1 with NAS fiscal cells. Mexico = ACS education weights only; Mariel stack calibrates <HS wage sensitivity band.

parameter_claims.population: native male workers by education-experience cell, national 1960-2000
parameter_claims.page_ref: p.2 abstract; p.14 Table III (θ=-0.572 → ~4% at 10% shock)
parameter_claims.notes: Treats national education×experience cells as closed markets; paper argues local spatial correlations cluster near zero because of adjustment. Unnamed: wage loss ≈ lifetime fiscal loss for <HS cells.
Geographic clustering motivates local-vs-national identification split relevant to PUMA fiscal attribution.
40-60% of measured local wage impact attenuated by native in/out-migration (state vs MSA definition). Critical for state/PUMA fiscal ledger: remaining incumbents ≠ shock population.

parameter_claims.population: working-age immigrants vs 0.337 natives in same six states
parameter_claims.notes: Extreme geographic concentration — local fiscal shocks and native out-migration co-locate (CA/NY/TX/FL/IL/NJ).
Canonical low-skill supply shock; Cuban labor force +20%. Null wage result for non-Cubans is lower bound for <HS cell calibration.
Card finds virtually no wage/unemployment effect on less-skilled non-Cubans. Unnamed: short-run Miami null generalizes to national <HS lifetime NPV.

parameter_claims.population: non-Hispanic men 25-59 HS dropouts, Miami vs employment placebo
parameter_claims.notes: Borjas rejoinder: -15.3% (SE 0.060) immediate ORG effect; -13.1% with Peri-Yasenov placebo. Upper-bound stress test for <HS wage path.

parameter_claims.population: race-adjusted low-skill Miami wage vs other MSAs
parameter_claims.notes: Race composition bias (black share spike in 1980 CPS) does not eliminate post-Mariel wage drop; full recovery by 1990. Episodic not permanent <HS earnings hit.
Peri-Sparber cite Borjas 4-5% loss as pessimistic anchor; their task-specialization model simulates 'quite small' net effect after native reallocation to interactive tasks.
Short-run -0.7%, long-run +0.3% for no-degree natives; average native +0.6% long run. Assumes high σ_LL between dropouts and HS grads — dilutes competition.

parameter_claims.population: natives vs immigrants within education-experience cell
parameter_claims.notes: Imperfect substitutability (σ≈20) generates complementarity channel; inverse elasticity ~0.05 in preferred specs.

parameter_claims.population: less-educated native workers, national factor-supply model
parameter_claims.notes: +1.7% to +2.6% wage effect 2000-2019; post-2000 immigration more college-skilled, net <HS flow negative — challenges extrapolating 1980s pessimism to Mexico-recent-surge cells.

parameter_claims.population: less-educated Danish natives, refugee dispersal quasi-IV 1991-2008
parameter_claims.notes: Low-skilled refugee inflow → natives shift to non-manual tasks; positive/null wages and employment. External-validity gap for US <HS but supports complementarity band in lifetime earnings projection.

parameter_claims.population: black men by skill group, 1960-2000 census
parameter_claims.notes: 10% immigrant supply shock → -4.0% black wage, -3.5pp employment, +0.8pp incarceration. Distributional cost absent from NAS average-native fiscal ledger.

parameter_claims.population: metro-area skill cells, Borjas immigrant-share specification
parameter_claims.notes: 100 immigrants → net -66 natives (Borjas pit spec). Preferred mit spec: only ~10% undone. Debate over attenuation magnitude directly affects who remains in PUMA to pay local taxes.

generators.name: Native out-migration fiscal residue
generators.prompt: For each immigration shock geography (state, MSA, PUMA), ask: what fraction of the measured local wage/fiscal burden is attenuated because natives left (Borjas w11610 40-60% band)? Who remains to pay state/local taxes and use schools — incumbents, in-migrants, or replacement population? Build a sensitivity table: full shock vs attenuated shock on origin×PUMA ledger.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced w11610 attenuation, Card-Peri JEL Table 3 mobility debate, and brainstorm Round 8 note on PUMA fiscal attribution before treating ACS snapshot populations as shock bearers.
generators.negative_space: Standing NAS/SIPP frame assigns costs to immigrants present in a cell without modeling native selective exit from high-immigrant PUMAs.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrants and incumbents co-located at shock time bear equal fiscal weight
Local wage impact measured in cross-section equals fiscal incidence on stayers
generators.theory_tested: Native out-migration attenuates local fiscal attribution by 40-60%

generators.name: Task complementarity vs substitution band
generators.prompt: When national estimates show large wage losses for <HS (Borjas) but area studies show nulls (Card), search for task-specialization or native-immigrant imperfect substitution mechanisms (Peri-Sparber, Ottaviano-Peri, Foged-Peri). Map the implied earnings-path band to indirect fiscal offsets (Colas-Sachs-style) rather than point-estimate NAS <HS NPV.
generators.retrodiction: Would have connected Peri-Sparber w13389, Foged-Peri w19315, Ottaviano-Peri w14188, and Card-Peri w32389 into a single complementarity sensitivity band before treating Borjas -4% as the earnings anchor.
generators.negative_space: Canonical CES perfect substitution within education cell — no task reallocation, no σ_IMMI heterogeneity.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Immigrants and natives with same education are perfect substitutes
Area-level null wage effects are measurement failure rather than complementarity
generators.theory_tested: Task complementarity compresses <HS wage losses into small positive long-run band

generators.name: Mariel <HS calibration bracket
generators.prompt: Treat Mariel boatlift as mandatory stress-test for <HS lifetime cell calibration: Card null (7% labor force, ~20% less-skilled supply) vs Borjas -13% to -15% immediate log wage vs race-adjusted recovery by 1990. Require any <HS NPV projection to declare where it sits in this bracket and why.
generators.retrodiction: Would have flagged Mariel stack (w3069, w21850, w23504) as the key falsifier for mapping wage pessimism into NAS -$109k <HS age-25 cell without sensitivity.
generators.negative_space: Single canonical wage elasticity applied to all low-skill inflows regardless of shock size, sample definition, or recovery horizon.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Mariel null result is representative of all <HS immigration
Mariel pessimistic replay is representative of all <HS immigration
generators.theory_tested: Mariel identifies bracket [0%, -15%] short-run <HS wage shock with full recovery within decade

generators.name: Wage effect ≠ fiscal effect
generators.prompt: Whenever a paper reports wage elasticity (e.g., 10% supply → 3-4% wage), ask what fiscal ledger line it actually moves: earnings tax base? transfer take-up? incarceration cost? school demand? Flag papers that infer fiscal sign from wage sign without PAYG, capital adjustment, or local cost channels.
generators.retrodiction: Would have blocked silent import of Borjas w9755 -4% into NAS <HS NPV without Storesletten/Clemens capital-tax and federal-offset disconfirmation passes.
generators.negative_space: Competitive labor market partial equilibrium — wage change is sufficient statistic for immigrant fiscal externality.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Lower native wages for <HS imply proportionally lower lifetime net fiscal contribution
National wage simulations translate directly to SIPP annual federal proxy cells
generators.theory_tested: Wage elasticities require separate mapping to tax, transfer, and local cost ledgers

generators.name: Sample-specification audit for <HS cells
generators.prompt: Before accepting any <HS wage estimate, audit sample rules: age floor (exclude 16-18 enrolled students misclassified as dropouts), sex pooling, Hispanic inclusion in 'native' trends, placebo city choice. Re-run sensitivity on Mexico-weighted <HS cell with each contamination flagged.
generators.retrodiction: Would have caught Borjas w21850 critique of Peri-Yasenov 16-18 misclassification and Card-Peri JEL pit-vs-mit specification fork before locking calibration parameters.
generators.negative_space: Education bucket from CPS highest grade completed taken at face value without age/enrollment filters.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: 'No HS degree' in CPS equals labor-market <HS competitor
Pooled men-women samples represent Mariel-era low-skill male shock
generators.theory_tested: <HS wage estimates are fragile to sample construction choices

generators.name: Distributional spillover beyond average native
generators.prompt: Search for immigration effects on subgroups omitted from average-native fiscal cells: Black employment/incarceration (Borjas-Grogger-Hanson), young/low-tenure manual workers (Foged-Peri). Ask whether lifetime ledger needs subgroup rows, not just education×state averages.
generators.retrodiction: Would have surfaced w12518 incarceration channel and Foged-Peri young-worker upgrading as missing ledger lines in Mexico <HS scalar debates.
generators.negative_space: Representative native worker sufficient for fiscal incidence; no racial or tenure heterogeneity.
unnamed_assumptions_surfaced: Average native wage effect captures fiscal incidence on vulnerable subpopulations
Incarceration and employment exits are non-fiscal social costs
generators.theory_tested: Immigration labor spillovers concentrate on specific native subgroups absent from NAS averages

theories_tested.theory: Native out-migration attenuates measured local wage/fiscal shock by 40-60%
theories_tested.prediction: PUMAs with high immigrant inflow and high native outflow show lower incumbent-household federal proxy than naive immigrant-count attribution implies
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Join origin_puma_household_stage5_context_2023 immigrant share delta to IRS SOI migration panel outflow rates by state; stratify PUMAs by Borjas-style attenuation quartile; compare sipp_scenario_ledger federal balance for incumbents vs all households
theories_tested.falsifier: No correlation between native net out-migration and immigrant share once state FE applied; attenuation parameter indistinguishable from 0

theories_tested.theory: Task complementarity yields small positive long-run <HS native wage effect post-2000
theories_tested.prediction: Occupations with rising immigrant manual-task share show native shift to interactive tasks and non-negative wage trend in QCEW sector cells mapped to education_bucket
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Crosswalk QCEW industry wages (lifetime stack) to acs education cells; correlate Mexican-origin <HS weight × task-interactive occupation share with annual federal microsim earnings band
theories_tested.falsifier: Card-Peri +1.7-2.6% band fails to appear in occupation-upgraded cells; Borjas -4% national elasticity dominates in 2000-2019 slice

theories_tested.theory: Mariel bracket bounds <HS lifetime earnings shock severity
theories_tested.prediction: Short-run earnings haircut for Mexico <HS recent-surge cell should not exceed ~15% unless explicitly assuming Borjas Mariel replay; long-run NPV should allow decade recovery
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Scenario compose: apply {0%, -4%, -15%} wage shock to acs_origin_household_federal_microsim <HS donor cells; compare NPV delta to NAS -$109k benchmark under SSA discount band
theories_tested.falsifier: SIPP-implied annual Mexico <HS proxy already embeds shock larger than Mariel upper bound without declaration

theories_tested.theory: Wage elasticity does not map 1:1 to fiscal NPV sign
theories_tested.prediction: Same wage shock produces different federal ledger sign when capital-tax adjustment (Clemens) or indirect spillover (Colas-Sachs) parameters applied vs raw earnings tax mapping
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Pipe borjas_w9755 elasticity (-0.04 per 10% supply) through federal microsim vs Clemens capital-tax multiplier on same donor cell; require explicit bridge table wage_delta → tax_delta ≠ 1:1
theories_tested.falsifier: Federal microsim tax response equals wage elasticity × statutory rate with R²≈1 across education buckets

theories_tested.theory: Black employment/incarceration spillover is a separate fiscal line from average native wage
theories_tested.prediction: States with high low-skill immigration × high Black share show incarceration/criminal-justice costs not captured in NAS education NPV rows
theories_tested.duckdb_test: Join borjas_grogger_hanson skill-group shocks to state incarceration spend proxies in stage5 context; flag states where distributional cost exceeds federal microsim offset for same immigrant cell
theories_tested.falsifier: No incremental state/local cost in high-Black-share PUMAs after controlling for immigrant education mix
